era: 1988-present. Grunge. E=8. Central District, Seattle. Sub Pop Records (primary). Reprise 1992-1998. The band that stayed.
key_albums: Touch Me I'm Sick single (1988, Sub Pop #18) -- first grunge single
Superfuzz Bigmuff EP (1988, Sub Pop) -- EP that exported Sub Pop grunge internationally
Mudhoney LP (1989, Sub Pop) -- debut album
Every Good Boy Deserves Fudge (1991, Sub Pop) -- pre-major lo-fi peak
Piece of Cake (1992, Reprise) -- major label debut
My Brother the Cow (1995, Reprise) -- Reprise peak
The Lucky Ones (2008, Sub Pop) -- best late-period
Digital Garbage (2018, Sub Pop) -- political, loud
Plastic Eternity (2023, Sub Pop) -- most recent

theory_nodes.concept: GREEN RIVER SPLIT (1988): Arm + Turner chose noise/fuzz direction -> Mudhoney. Ament + Gossard chose melodic/commercial direction -> Mother Love Bone -> Pearl Jam. The most productive single dissolution in Seattle grunge history. Both directions essential.
theory_nodes.description: Green River split as the fork producing Mudhoney and (indirectly) Pearl Jam.

theory_nodes.concept: Touch Me I'm Sick (Sub Pop SP18, August 1988): the first true grunge single. Released same month Sub Pop launched. The single and the label define each other. Big Muff Pi + sneer-and-howl vocal = grunge form realized.
theory_nodes.description: Touch Me I'm Sick as grunge's foundational single document.

theory_nodes.concept: Superfuzz Bigmuff EP (Sub Pop, October 1988): named after guitar effects (Univox Super-Fuzz + Electro-Harmonix Big Muff Pi). The primary international export of Sub Pop grunge. European music press (NME, Melody Maker) reviews built the cult. Created the international Seattle myth before Nevermind.
theory_nodes.description: Superfuzz Bigmuff as the EP that exported grunge internationally.

theory_nodes.concept: THE BAND THAT STAYED. Nirvana to DGC. Soundgarden to A&M. Pearl Jam to Epic. TAD to East West. Mudhoney to Reprise (1992-1998), then back to Sub Pop. The longest-running original Sub Pop act. The label's emblem of scene commitment.
theory_nodes.description: Mudhoney as Sub Pop's longest-running act and emblem of original-scene commitment.

theory_nodes.concept: Nirvana covered Touch Me I'm Sick on the 1990 Mudhoney/Nirvana split single. Mudhoney covered Parliament-Funkadelic's Maggot Brain. The exchange documents the Cobain-Arm peer relationship and Nirvana's engagement with the Sub Pop aesthetic.
theory_nodes.description: Nirvana-Mudhoney split single as peer documentation.

cross_references.relationship: Mudhoney's genesis. Arm + Turner from Green River split 1988. The noise/fuzz direction vs. the melodic direction. Both outcomes essential to the engine.
E=8 Summit companion. Both Sub Pop grunge at E=8. Different registers: TAD low-end density vs. Mudhoney fuzz-attitude. Both essential to the Summit's E=8 entry zone.
Nirvana covered Touch Me I'm Sick (1990 split single). Cobain-Arm peer relationship. Both Sub Pop; Nirvana left for DGC. Mudhoney ultimately stayed.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Exact energy match. Multiple overlapping voices in driving rain conditions. Mudhoney's multiple simultaneous fuzz guitars through the Sub Pop noise storm. Both at E=8 through simultaneous layered voices.
